title: [Kconfig:36-40] RX thread priority has no range constraint
description:
Round 4: CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2_RX_PRIORITY has no 'range' constraint in Kconfig. A user could set priority to any int value (e.g., -1 or 256). Zephyr thread priorities have valid ranges (typically 0 to CONFIG_NUM_PREEMPT_PRIORITIES-1 for preemptible threads). Setting an invalid priority passed to k_thread_create() at lora_l2.c:388 could cause undefined scheduler behavior - thread may not run, scheduler may corrupt internal state, or kernel panic on debug builds.

Fix: Add 'range 0 <max>' to Kconfig where max depends on scheduler config. Common safe max is 14 or 15. Example:
  range 0 14
close_reason: Fixed: Added range constraint (0 to NUM_PREEMPT_PRIORITIES) for RX thread priority config
